{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":262749,"duration_api_ms":262369,"ttft_ms":2943,"num_turns":33,"result":"Review complete. **Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** — does not block pack or goal closeout.

CP00-481 (descriptor-only RP16 governance-core model-foundation slice, 40 units, risk B) is internally consistent across registry binding, contract, manifest, baseline, and the closeout-pack-plan. The descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data boundaries hold, `production_ready:false` is honest (no overclaim), Claude's read-only non-approver role is preserved, and Hermes patterns are evidence-only (not product runtime).

Two cosmetic **P3** findings, both non-blocking:
- `model.test.js:113` — test title says "RP15" instead of "RP16" (assertions are correct).
- `registry.js:357` — CP481 no-write attestation inherits the scope-contract-foundation flag alongside the model-foundation-slice flag (internally consistent, harmless).

No P0/P1/P2 issues. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claims are supported: (1) Coverage reconciles exactly across registry binding (packages/governance/src/registry.js:18-33), contract current_pack/historical_pack_bindings, manifest, baseline, and the closeout-pack-plan pack — 40 contiguous units ending at M04.S06 with deliverable split {implementation:23,ui:5,fixture:2,test:6,hermes_evidence:2,claude_review:2} and micro-phase counts M02:12/M03:22/M04:6. (2) Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: all generated results are frozen with writes_product_state:false, loads_real_fixture_data:false, dispatches_*_runtime:false, and the validators enforce descriptor_only, runtime_execution:false, cross-tenant denial, and the seven no-leak guards; test/validator code only reads JSON (no writes), and the package directory contains only descriptor source (no runtime, no fixtures with real data). (3) production_ready is honestly false (manifest status implemented_pending_review; gate_outcome implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending) — no overclaim. (4) Authority boundary preserved: claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false; review-request is read-only with Read/Grep/Glob and the readiness auth-preflight returned valid non-empty JSON (exit 0). (5) Hermes patterns are descriptor/evidence-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. The handoff (next_subphase RP16.P01.M04.S07) matches CP00-482's first unit. Only two cosmetic P3 findings; neither blocks pack or goal closeout. Note: command-evidence reports all validation/test gates at exit 0, which this reviewer cannot re-execute (read-only); inspection confirms the source supports those claims and that post-review-gated artifacts (raw-output/receipt) are expected to be produced after this run.","findings":[{"id":"CP00-481-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"Test title references wrong program (RP15 instead of RP16)","file":"packages/governance/test/model.test.js","line":113,"details":"The test description string reads "CP00-481 plan binding covers the planned 40 RP15 model foundation slice units" but CP00-481 belongs to program RP16 (Governance DLP Retention), not RP15 (Analytics). The test's assertions are correct (they check RP16/CP00-481 bindings, unit_count 40, and RP16.P01.M02/M03/M04 micro-phase counts), so this is purely a cosmetic label error with no functional impact.","recommendation":"Change "RP15" to "RP16" in the test title for accuracy. For a model-foundation-slice pack the retained scope-contract-foundation flag is slightly redundant/misleading. It is internally consistent (the contract deep-equals the JS object, which the validator asserts) and harmless — additive descriptor flags only — so it does not affect any guard or boundary.","recommendation":"Optionally drop the inherited validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only flag from the CP481 attestation (or rename to a cumulative lineage flag) so each pack's attestation names only its own validated descriptor.
